mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the broader macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), it signals an accelerating labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances where the Sahm-style UNRATE trigger fires, or the trigger fires fewer than 3 times in the available sample, precluding meaningful statistical testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) growth and rising HOUST (housing starts) jointly indicate strengthening consumer and residential investment demand that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as aggregate demand expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and HOUST 3-month growth > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST positive momentum signal fails to outperform a single-series RSAFS-only signal by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) in walk-forward backtests, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by elevated FEDFUNDS, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals supply-side capacity constraints or demand exhaustion that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during high-FEDFUNDS periods (>4%) is not associated with negative 60-day forward SPX returns at a rate exceeding 55%, or the relationship reverses sign in more than one of three historical restrictive-policy episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains above 3%, the policy regime is classified as restrictive-and-inflation-fighting; equity markets price in continued tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation shows sustained deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 320.321 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: restrictive monetary tightening with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns during periods meeting both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL thresholds are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) compared to unconditional baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market strength and consumer income generation, providing a short-horizon positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as it confirms near-term economic resilience ahead of earnings and consumption data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159517 thousand as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or PAYEMS revisions (high revision risk) cause sign flips in more than 20% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive but RSAFS monthly growth simultaneously turns negative, it signals an emerging divergence between aggregate output and consumer spending that foreshadows a demand slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the divergence typically resolves through downward GDP revisions.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 0 and RSAFS 3-month growth < 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23526.085 billion as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence signal fails to precede negative 60-day forward SPX returns in more than half of historical occurrences, or GDPC1 high revision risk causes the trigger to be non-reproducible in real-time vintage data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO that crosses from positive to negative growth signals an inflection into manufacturing contraction, which historically leads S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 20 trading days as industrial weakness feeds into broader earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth crosses from positive to negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.8799 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following an INDPRO positive-to-negative growth crossing are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to unconditional baseline, or the crossing signal produces contradictory results in more than 40% of historical instances.
